risinfo.chaptertitle: 18. Common Surgical Procedures and Sedation in Facility Settings

sect1.title: Percutaneous Transcatheter Closure of Patent Ductus Arteriosus
sect1.para: 93582 Percutaneous transcatheter closure of patent ductus arteriosus

listitem.para: Includes, when performed (do not separately report)
Introduction of catheter, right heart or main pulmonary artery (36013) — Selective catheter placement, left or right pulmonary artery (36014)
Introduction of catheter, aorta (36200) — Aortography, thoracic, without serialography, radiological supervision and interpretation (75600)
Aortography, thoracic, by serialography, radiological supervision and interpretation (75605) — Heart catheterization or catheter placement for coronary angiography (93451–94361)

Heart catheterization for congenital cardiac anomalies (93593–93598) — Injection procedure during cardiac catheterization including imaging supervision, interpretation, and report; for supravalvular aortography (93567)
Separately report when performed at the time of transcatheter patent ductus arteriosus closure
Pharmacologic agent administration (93463) — Intracardiac echocardiography during therapeutic/diagnostic intervention, including imaging supervision and interpretation (93662)

AOCs for injection procedures during cardiac catheterization, including imaging supervision, with interpretation and report, for ◊ Selective coronary angiography during congenital heart catheterization (93563)

sect1.para: ◊ Selective opacification of aortocoronary venous or arterial bypass graft(s) to one or more coronary arteries and insitu arterial conduits, whether native or used for bypass to one or more coronary arteries during congenital heart catheterization, when performed (93564) ◊ Selective left ventricular or left atrial angiography (93565)

◊ Selective right ventricular or right atrial angiography (93566) ◊ Nonselective pulmonary arterial angiography (93568) or selective pulmonary angiography (93569, 93573–93575) performed during congenital heart catheterization
Modifier 63 may be appended to 93582 when the procedure is performed on an infant weighing less than 4 kg.

sect2.title: Vascular Access
sect2.para: Many vascular access procedures are included in the services reported with critical or intensive care codes. Refer to Chapter 17 for a list of procedures not separately reported by a physician or QHP who is reporting critical care services on the same date.

sect4.title: Central Venous Access
sect4.para: There is no distinction between venous access achieved percutaneously or via cutdown. Report the venous access code appropriate to the ● Type of insertion (ie, centrally inserted or peripherally inserted)

listitem.para: Type of catheter (eg, non-tunneled or tunneled)
Device (eg, with or without port)
Age of child Innon-facility settings, 96522 may be reported for refilling and maintenance of an implantable pump or reservoir for systemic (intravenous, intra-arterial) drug delivery.

sect4.para: Irrigation of an implanted venous access device for drug delivery (96523) is reported only innon-facility settings and when no other service is reported by the same individual on the same date.
✖ Do not report codes for refilling and maintenance and/or irrigation by physicians in facility settings.

If an existing central venous access device is removed and a new one placed via a separate venous access site, appropriate codes for both procedures (removal and old, if code exists, and insertion of new device) should be reported. Refer to replacement codes for removal with replacement via the same venous access site.

sect4.title: Insertion or Replacement of Centrally Inserted Central Venous Access Device
sect4.para: Refer to "Centrally Inserted Central Venous Catheter: Codes for Insertion and Replacement" at www.aap.org/cfp2026 for a comparison of codes by procedure and the patient's age.

listitem.para: Codes 36555–36558 and 36560 and 36561 are selected according to the patient's age on the date of the procedure to insert the catheter.
Codes for insertion of a tunneled centrally inserted central venous catheter (36563–36566) are not selected according to the patient's age.

To qualify as a centrally inserted central venous access device, both the following circumstances must be true: — The entry site must be the jugular, subclavian, or femoral vein or the inferior vena cava.
The tip of the catheter must terminate in the subclavian, brachiocephalic (innominate), or iliac veins; the superior or inferior vena cava; or the right atrium.
Moderate sedation services are separately reportable with 99151–99157. For more information on reporting moderate sedation, refer to the Moderate Sedation section in Chapter 13.

Report imaging guidance for gaining access to venous entry site or manipulating the catheter into final central position (in addition to 36555–36558) with either of the following codes: — 76937 (ultrasound guidance for vascular access requiring ultrasound evaluation of potential access sites, documentation of selected vessel patency, concurrent real-time ultrasound visualization of vascular needle entry, with permanent recording and reporting)

77001 (fluoroscopic guidance for central venous access device placement, replacement [catheter only or complete], or removal [includes fluoroscopic guidance for vascular access and catheter manipulation, any necessary contrast injections through access site or catheter with related venography radiologic supervision and interpretation, and radiographic documentation of final catheter position]) ✱ Do not separately report ultrasonography when performed only for vessel identification.
Report replacement of the catheter only with 36578, or report complete replacement with 36578–36583.

sect4.title: Insertion of Peripherally Inserted Central Venous Access Device
sect4.para: Refer to "Peripherally Inserted Central Venous Catheter: Codes" at www.aap.org/cfp2026 for comparison of codes for PICC insertion procedures and codes for PICC replacement procedures.

listitem.para: To qualify as a PICC, both the following circumstances must be true: — The entry site must be a peripheral vein (eg, axillary, basilic, or cephalic).

The tip of the catheter must terminate in the subclavian, brachiocephalic (innominate), or iliac veins; the superior or inferior vena cava; or the right atrium. Midline catheters terminate in the peripheral venous system. Midline catheter insertion is not reported as a PICC insertion. Refer to 36400, 36406, and 36410 for midline catheter insertion. Note that 36400, 36406, and 36410 are not separately reported when the same physician or QHP is reporting neonatal or pediatric critical or intensive care services.

Codes 36568 and 36569 are reported for PICC insertion without a subcutaneous port and without imaging guidance according to the patient's age. This includes placement through magnetic guidance or any guidance modality that does not include imaging with image documentation.

sect4.para: ✱ Do not report imaging guidance (76937, 77001) in conjunction with 36568 and 36569.

listitem.para: If imaging guidance is used in PICC insertion, refer to 36572, 36573, and 36584. Imaging guidance is included in the placement (36572, 36573) or complete replacement (36584) of a PICC without subcutaneous port or pump.

sect4.para: ✱ Do not report chest radiographs (71045–71048) for the purpose of documenting the final catheter position by the same physician on the same date of service as the PICC insertion (36572, 36573, or 36584). If PICC insertion is performed without confirmation of the catheter tip location, append modifier 52 (reduced service) to the code for catheter insertion (36572, 36573, or 36584).

listitem.para: Documentation of services includes — Images from all modalities used (eg, ultrasonography, fluoroscopy) stored in the patient record
Associated supervision and interpretation — Venography performed through the same venous puncture
The final central position of the catheter with imaging ● Report PICC insertion with a subcutaneous port with 36570 and 36571.

Ultrasound (76937) or fluoroscopic guidance (77001) may be separately reported when performed and documented. Documentation of ultrasound guidance for PICC placement should include evaluation of the potential puncture sites, patency of the entry vein, and real-time ultrasound visualization of needle entry into the vein.

sect4.title: Repair of Central Venous Catheters
sect4.para: 36575 Repair of tunneled or non-tunneled central venous access catheter, without subcutaneous port or pump, central or peripheral insertion site 36576 Repair of central venous access device, with subcutaneous port or pump, central or peripheral insertion site

36593 Declotting by thrombolytic agent of implanted vascular access device or catheter 36595 Mechanical removal of pericatheter obstructive material (eg, fibrin sheath) from central venous device via separate venous access For radiological supervision and interpretation of mechanical removal of pericatheter obstructive material from a central venous device via separate venous access, report 75901.

36596 Mechanical removal of intraluminal (intracatheter) obstructive material from central venous device through device lumen 36598 Contrast injection(s) for radiologic evaluation of existing central venous access device, including fluoroscopy, image documentation and report
Repair of a central venous catheter includes fixing the device without replacement of the catheter or port/pump.

listitem.para: For the repair of a multi-catheter device, with or without subcutaneous ports or pumps, use the appropriate code describing the service with 2 units of service.
Repair of any central venous access catheter without a port or pump is reported with 36575.
Repair of any central venous access catheter with a port or pump is reported with 36576.
Code 36593 is reported for declotting of an implanted vascular device or catheter by thrombolysis.
Code 36593 is not reported for declotting of a pleural catheter. Use unlisted procedure code 32999 for declotting of a pleural catheter.
Codes 36595 and 36596 are reported for a procedure with mechanical removal of pericatheter or intraluminal obstructive material.
Radiological supervision and interpretation are separately reported with 75901.
✖ Do not report 36595 or 36596 in conjunction with 36593 or 36598.
When the patency of a central venous catheter is evaluated under fluoroscopy, 36598 is reported for the contrast injection, image documentation, and report.
Fluoroscopy (76000) is not separately reported.
Refer to 75820–75827 for complete venography studies.

sect4.title: Removal of Central Venous Catheters
sect4.para: 36589 Removal of tunneled central venous catheter, without subcutaneous port or pump 36590 Removal of tunneled central venous access device, with subcutaneous port or pump, central or peripheral insertion

Removal codes are reported for removal of the entire device. Refer to replacement codes for partial replacement (catheter only) or complete exchange in the same venous access site of central venous access devices.

listitem.para: For removal of both catheters (placed from separate venous access sites) of a multi-catheter device, with or without subcutaneous ports/pumps, use the appropriate code describing the procedure with 2 units of service.
When removal of an existing central venous access device is performed in conjunction with placement of a new device via a separate venous access site, report both procedures.

sect4.title: Arterial Access
sect4.para: 36600 Arterial puncture, withdrawal of blood for diagnosis 36620 Arterial catheterization or cannulation for sampling, monitoring or transfusion (separate procedure); percutaneous
36625 cutdown

listitem.para: An arterial puncture for diagnosis is reported with 36600.
Codes 36620 and 36625 differentiate reporting for peripheral arterial catheterization by percutaneous insertion from when a cutdown is performed.

sect2.title: Transfusions
sect2.para: 36430 Transfusion, blood or blood components 36440 Push transfusion; blood, 2 years or younger
Push transfusion (36440) is used only for patients younger than 3 years.

36455 other than newborn 36456 Partial exchange transfusion, blood, plasma, or crystalloid necessitating the skill of a physician or other QHP; newborn The placement of catheters to support exchange transfusions may be reported separately with the appropriate vascular access codes.

listitem.para: Transfusion (36430) or push transfusion (36440) should be used only if the physician personally infuses the substance and not if the blood is administered by nursing personnel and allowed to enter the vessel via gravity ormeter flow. Codes 36430 and 36440 are not separately reported when the same physician or physicians of the same specialty and same group practice are reporting neonatal or pediatric critical care (99468–99472, 99475, 99476).
Complete exchange transfusions (double volume) performed during the neonatal period are reported with 36450; exchange transfusions for all other age groups are reported with 36455.
The assumption is that the exchange for the neonate is performed via the umbilical vein, whereas an exchange for an older child or adult is performed via a peripheral vessel.
The actual placement of catheters to support the exchange transfusion may be reported separately with the appropriate vascular access codes.
Partial exchange transfusions (eg, for hyperviscosity syndrome in the neonate) should be reported with 36456.

sect2.para: ✱ Do not report other transfusion codes (36430, 36440, or 36450) with 36456.
✱ Do not append modifier 63 (procedure performed on infants <4 kg) when reporting 36450 or 36456.
Diagnosis codes Z67.R1–Z67.R4 are reported when a patient's Duffy phenotype influences MDM related to transfusions.

sect2.title: Ear, Nose, and Throat Procedures
sect2.para: 69421 Myringotomy including aspiration and/or eustachian tube inflation requiring general anesthesia 69433 Tympanostomy (requiring insertion of ventilating tube), local or topical anesthesia
69436 Tympanostomy (requiring insertion of ventilating tube), general anesthesia

listitem.para: For bilateral procedure, report code 69436 with modifier 50. Refer to Chapter 2 for further discussion of modifier 50.
Insertion of ventilating tube(s) is not separately reported when performed in conjunction with another middle ear procedure (eg, tympanoplasty) without significant increase in work.
Cleaning debris from the lateral drum, including a pars flaccida retraction pocket, is inclusive to the tube placement code.
Use of an operating microscope is included in the procedure described by 69436.

sect2.para: Code 69436 may be reported without a modifier (eg, 52, reduced service) when a ventilating tube is replaced under general anesthesia. Report 69433 (tympanostomy requiring insertion of a ventilation tube) for replacement under local or topical anesthesia.

sect4.title: Nasopharyngoscopy
sect4.para: 92511 Nasopharyngoscopy with endoscope (separate procedure) Code 92511 is designated as a "separate procedure" and should not be reported in addition to the code for the total procedure or service of which it is considered an integral component.

listitem.para: When carried out independently, report 92511.
When considered to be unrelated or distinct from other procedures or services provided at that time, report 92511 59 in addition to other procedures or services.

sect2.title: Laryngoplasty
sect2.para: #31551 Laryngoplasty; for laryngeal stenosis, with graft, without indwelling stent placement, younger than 12 years of age
#31553 Laryngoplasty; for laryngeal stenosis, with graft, with indwelling stent placement, younger than 12 years of age
#31554 age 12 years or older 31580 Laryngoplasty; for laryngeal web, with indwelling keel or stent insertion

31584 Laryngoplasty; with open reduction and fixation of (eg, plating) fracture, includes tracheostomy, if performed ✱ Do not report the graft separately if harvested through the laryngoplasty incision (eg, thyroid cartilage graft).

listitem.para: Report only 1 of the following codes for a single operative session: 31551-31554 or 31580.

Codes for treatment of laryngeal stenosis by laryngoplasty with the graft are selected depending on whether the procedure includes indwelling stent placement and on the age of the patient (<12 years or ≥12 years).
Open treatment of a hyoid fracture is reported with 31584 (repair procedure on the larynx).
To report tracheostomy, refer to 31600, 31601, 31603, 31605, or 31610.
Report laryngoplasty, not otherwise specified or for removal of a keel or stent, with 31599.

sect4.title: Adenoidectomy and Tonsillectomy
sect4.para: 42820 Tonsillectomy and adenoidectomy; younger than age 12
42821 age 12 or over 42825 Tonsillectomy, primary or secondary; younger than age 12
42826 age 12 or over
42830 Adenoidectomy, primary; younger than age 12
42831 age 12 or over
42835 Adenoidectomy, secondary; younger than age 12
42836 age 12 or over Report only the code for the combined tonsillectomy and adenoidectomy (42820–42821) when both are provided.
For partial ablation of the tonsils with a laser, report the appropriate code from 42820 or 42821, or 42825 or 42826, with modifier 52 (reduced service).

For control of hemorrhage following tonsillectomy or adenoidectomy, refer to 42960–42972. Modifier 22 (increased service) may be reported in conjunction with tonsillectomy codes when significant bleeding during the procedure requires extensive dissection.

⚹ Do not append modifier 52 (reduced service) when intracapsular tonsillectomy is performed. Report the procedure (tonsillectomy or tonsillectomy with adenoidectomy) with the appropriate code without a modifier, as the work of the procedure and outcome are unchanged.
⚹ Do not separately report incision and drainage of a peritonsillar abscess (42700) when performed in conjunction with tonsillectomy.

sect4.title: Gastric Intubation and Aspiration
sect4.para: 43752 Naso- or orogastric tube placement, requiring physician's skill and fluoroscopic guidance (includes fluoroscopy, image documentation and report) 43753 Gastric intubation and aspiration(s) therapeutic (eg, for ingested poisons), including lavage if performed

Do not report 43752 or 43753 in conjunction with critical or intensive care services (99291, 99292; 99466–99469; 99471–99476; or 99477–99480). Append modifier 25 to codes for other significant, separately identifiable E/M services (eg, hospital care, consultation).

Note that 43752 is reported when a physician's skill and fluoroscopic guidance are required for placement of a nasogastric or orogastric tube. Use of fluoroscopic guidance must be documented (ie, image documentation and report).
⚹ Do not separately report 43752 for tube placement to insufflate the stomach before percutaneous gastrointestinal tube placement.
⚹ Do not report 43752 when a physician's skill is not required or placement is performed without fluoroscopic guidance.

Examples ▶ A patient requires placement of a nasogastric tube. Attempts by nursing staff are unsuccessful, so placement by the physician is required. The physician places the tube using fluoroscopic guidance.
▶A child is brought to the ED after having ingested drugs. Gastric intubation and lavage are performed.
Code 43753 is reported in addition to the appropriate E/M service (eg, ED services [99281–99285]).

sect4.title: Appendectomy
sect4.para: 44950 Appendectomy ♦44955 when done for indicated purpose at time of other major procedure
44960 for ruptured appendix with abscess or generalized peritonitis
44970 Laparoscopy, surgical, appendectomy
44979 Unlisted laparoscopy procedure, appendix As a sole procedure, appendectomy coding is fairly straightforward, with 3 code choices: 44950, 44960, and 44970.
CPT instructs that incidental appendectomy (ie, not caused by disease or symptom) during intra-abdominal surgery does not usually warrant reporting a code for the appendectomy procedure.

listitem.para: If it is necessary to report an incidental appendectomy performed during another intra-abdominal procedure, append modifier 52 (reduced service).

The Medicaid NCCI manual also instructs that incidental removal of a normal appendix during another abdominal surgery is not separately reportable. This instruction is likely followed by other payers.

sect4.para: Appendectomy performed for an indicated purpose (ie, problem with the appendix) in conjunction with another procedure is separately reported.

listitem.para: Report AOC 44955 in addition to the code for the primary procedure when open appendectomy is performed (because clinically indicated) in conjunction with another procedure.
For a laparoscopic appendectomy when done for an indicated purpose at the time of another major procedure, report 44979.

A separate diagnosis code (distinct from the diagnoses for which other intra-abdominal procedures were performed) should be linked to the appendectomy procedure code to indicate the medical necessity of the procedure.

sect4.title: Inguinal Hernia Repair
sect4.para: Diagnosis codes for reporting an inguinal hernia are shown in Table 18-3. Report additional codes that specify preterm birth/gestational age (P07.3-, additional character required to designate completed weeks of gestation) and/or comorbidities that affect patient management in addition to codes for inguinal hernia.

sect4.title: Laparoscopic Inguinal Hernia Repair
sect4.para: For laparoscopic inguinal hernia repair, report either 49650 or 49651. Append modifier 50 (bilateral procedure) to the code for inguinal hernia repair performed bilaterally. Modifier 63 (procedure performed in infants less than 4 kg) may be appended to codes 49650–49651 when the procedures are performed in neonates and infants up to a present body weight of 4 kg.
49650 Laparoscopy, surgical; repair initial inguinal hernia
49651 repair recurrent inguinal hernia

sect4.title: Open Inguinal Hernia Repair
sect4.para: For open hernia repair, refer to the procedure codes in Table 18-4.

listitem.para: For the purpose of reporting inguinal hernia repair codes 49491–49492 and 49495–49496, selection is based, in part, on preterm or full-term birth andage at the time of the procedure. For preterm neonates and infants, postconception age equals gestational age at birth plus age of newborn/infant in weeks at the time of the hernia repair.

sect4.title: Table 18-4. Codes for Open Repair of Inguinal Hernia in Infants and Children

row.entry: Infant's Birth Status (preterm/term) and Age at Time of Surgery
Term 50 wk PCage and <6 mo old—initial repair
Any infant 6 mo to <5 y old—initial repair

sect4.para: Abbreviation: PC, postconception.
1 Procedures reported with codes 49491–49501 include hydrocelectomy, when performed.
2 Excision or repair of strangulated organ or structure (eg, 44120) is reported in addition to the repair of a strangulated hernia.
3 Separately report simple orchiopexy (54520) or excision of hydrocele (55040 or 55041), when performed, in conjunction with 49505 or 49507.
Examples of Calculating Postconception Age ▶ Postconception age up to 50 weeks (eg, 35-weeks'–gestational age infant now 6 weeks old = 41 weeks' postconception age)

▶ Postconception age greater than 50 weeks (eg, 35-weeks'–gestational age infant now 16 weeks old = 51 weeks' postconception age) ✱ Do not append modifier 63 (procedure performed on infants <4 kg) to 49491, 49492, 49495, or 49496.

listitem.para: Separately report inguinal hernia repair (49495–49525) performed in conjunction with inguinal orchiopexy (54640).
Append modifier 50 when procedures are performed bilaterally.

sect4.para: Refer to 49591–49618 for reporting anterior abdominal hernia(s) (ie, epigastric, incisional, ventral, umbilical, spigelian) and 49621 or 49622 for reporting repair of parastomal hernia.
